mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month decline in FEDFUNDS reflects an active monetary easing cycle that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days on average across the sample.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS fell from above 5% in mid-2007 to 2.98% by Feb 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or mean forward return in declining-rate periods is not significantly greater than in flat/rising-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year contraction in housing starts (HOUST) signals a deepening residential investment collapse that propagates into broader demand destruction and household balance-sheet stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1012K units in Jan 2008, down sharply from 2006 peaks near 2000K
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following a >20% YoY HOUST decline are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS month-over-month decline) and an upward tick in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly confirm a genuine labor market inflection that individually noisy single-series moves do not, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises by at least 0.1pp in the same reporting month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137993K in Feb 2008 with early signs of job losses; UNRATE at 4.8%
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS decline + UNRATE rise signal produces forward 20d returns that are not significantly more negative than either single-series signal alone (interaction effect not significant at p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During an acute financial-stress regime, aggressive FEDFUNDS rate cuts are interpreted by markets as confirmation of severe underlying credit and liquidity distress rather than stimulative relief, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days even as rates fall.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS cut of 50bp or more within a single FOMC action while credit-stress conditions are elevated
evidence_refs: fact:Bear Stearns near-collapse and emergency Fed liquidity actions occurring around March 2008
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following large emergency rate cuts during stress periods are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, reversing the hypothesized negative reaction.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent downtrend in industrial production (INDPRO) signals contracting manufacturing output and corporate earnings power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the market prices in weaker aggregate supply-side activity.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.2334 in Jan 2008, showing flattening momentum after 2007 peak
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or negative INDPRO trend periods do not show significantly lower mean returns than positive-trend periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in retail sales (RSAFS) indicate a weakening consumer demand base, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as consumption accounts for the majority of GDP and corporate revenue exposure.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month for two consecutive reporting months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 380197 million in Feb 2008 amid signs of consumer retrenchment
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following two-consecutive-month RSAFS declines are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Once UNRATE crosses above a critical threshold associated with historical recession onsets, markets reprice equities lower over the subsequent quarter as rising unemployment confirms a deteriorating earnings and consumption outlook, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% or increases by more than 0.5pp from its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% in Feb 2008, up from 4.4-4.5% lows in 2007
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after UNRATE crosses the threshold are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is being cut aggressively at the same time that PAYEMS is contracting, the joint signal indicates the Fed is reactively easing into a confirmed labor market recession rather than pre-emptively stimulating growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days despite falling rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS cumulative cut exceeds 100bp over 6 months AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month in at least 2 of the last 3 reports
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut rates from 5.25% to 2.98% between Sep 2007 and Feb 2008 while payroll growth stalled
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during joint aggressive-easing-plus-payroll-contraction episodes are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically distinguishable from the sum of individual FEDFUNDS and PAYEMS single-series effects (p > 0.10).
